title: The Policy and Value Networks
concept: PPO uses an Actor (the LLM generating text) and a Critic (estimating the expected reward of a partial response) working together.

exercises.prompt: What key components does PPO use to guide the LLM's learning process?

options.text: An Actor and a Critic network working together
A single network that does everything alone
A team of human editors correcting text in real-time

exercises.explain: PPO uses an Actor-Critic architecture where the Actor generates text and the Critic evaluates its quality.

cards.title: The AI Dynamic Duo
cards.body: How does an AI learn to write not just any text, but the absolute best text? It uses a clever buddy system! One network writes the draft, while another predicts how successful it will be.

cards.title: Actor and Critic Networks
cards.body: In Proximal Policy Optimization (PPO), we split the brain into two parts: the Policy (Actor) which generates the actual words, and the Value Network (Critic) which estimates the expected score or reward of the text generated so far.

cards.title: The Comedian and the Coach
cards.body: Think of it like a stand-up comedian and a comedy coach. The Actor is the comedian on stage, trying out new jokes (generating text). The Critic is the coach in the back room, guessing how well the rest of the set will go based on the first few jokes.

cards.title: Cooperating in Real-Time
cards.body: When writing a story, the Actor suggests the next word: "Once... upon...". Meanwhile, the Critic looks at "Once upon" and estimates that this story has a high chance of getting a good reward from the user.

cards.title: Does the Critic Write?
cards.body: People often think the Critic edits or rewrites the text directly. It doesn't! The Critic only acts as a judge, predicting the future score so the Actor knows how to improve its writing style.

cards.title: Roles at a Glance
cards.body: PPO divides the workload between generation and evaluation to train the model effectively.

left.text: Generates the actual words and steps of the text response.

right.text: Estimates the future reward of the generated draft.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary job of the Actor (Policy) network in PPO?

options.text: Generating the actual text or actions
Calculating the exact final score from the human user
Storing the database of training prompts
Stopping the training process when it gets bored

exercises.explain: The Actor is the policy network responsible for choosing actions (generating the next tokens or words).

exercises.prompt: True or False: The Critic's job is to provide the final human reward score for the finished text.
exercises.explain: The Critic does not give the final feedback; it estimates the expected future reward of partial text to guide the Actor.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing network role in this PPO setup:
exercises.sentence: While the Actor generates the token, the [blank] estimates the expected reward.
exercises.explain: The Critic (Value Network) evaluates the current state by predicting the expected future reward, helping the Actor improve.

exercises.prompt: If you observe an LLM actively generating a sentence word-by-word during PPO training, which network is doing the generation?

options.text: The Critic is generating the text response.
The Actor is generating the text response.
An external human is typing the response in real-time.

exercises.explain: The Critic only evaluates the quality and predicts the reward; it is the Actor that actually generates the text response.

exercises.prompt: You are designing a chatbot using PPO. During a conversation, how do the Policy (Actor) and Value Network (Critic) split the work?

options.text: The Actor evaluates rewards and the Critic generates the text.
The Actor decides what words to say, while the Critic estimates the future reward of those choices.
Both networks generate different parts of the same sentence simultaneously.
The Critic blocks bad words from being generated by the Actor.

exercises.explain: The Actor (Policy) focuses on generating the action or text, while the Critic (Value Network) evaluates how good that state is by estimating the expected future reward.
